steps.title: Support the polar-range claim
steps.content: The researchers claim endothermy enabled mosasaurs to include relatively cold polar waters in their range. Strong support should show mosasaurs in polar regions where nonendothermic reptiles were less common.

steps.title: Reject weak support
steps.content:
• Choice A: Ease of measuring body temperature does not show where mosasaurs lived.
  • Choice B: Equal fossil numbers would undercut the idea that endothermy gave mosasaurs a polar advantage.
  • Choice D: Warmer-than-present seas do not directly compare mosasaurs with nonendothermic reptiles.

steps.title: Use fossil contrast at the poles
steps.content: Finding several mosasaur fossils near ancient poles but relatively few nonendothermic reptile fossils there supports endothermy as the enabling difference. The answer is C.
